scenarios.title: Rule-Changing Offensive: SAP as the AI Data Context Unifier
scenarios.goal: Proactively redefine the enterprise AI market by making SAP's platform the central hub for all enterprise data context, not just SAP data.
scenarios.target: Market leadership in business AI by capturing the data-context-first shift.
scenarios.objective: Integrate Reltio to create a breakthrough, asymmetric advantage in AI-ready data, forcing competitors to play catch-up in external data integration.

variables.name: Speed of Reltio integration into BDC
variables.description: How quickly SAP can embed Reltio's AI-native MDM as the core of its data fabric.

variables.name: Customer adoption of new migration model
variables.description: Percentage of customers accepting a de-risked, success-based migration fee structure.

variables.name: External data source connectivity
variables.description: Number and strategic importance of non-SAP applications and data platforms integrated via the enhanced BDC.

constraints: Must maintain Reltio's cloud-native approach to data integration and MDM
Must leverage Reltio's AI-based entity resolution system for golden records
Must integrate data from outside SAP's broad application portfolio
tradeoff_pressure: Pressure to move fast vs. risk of botched integration that reinforces negative customer perceptions.
Pressure to offer aggressive migration guarantees vs. protecting near-term revenue from existing BDC contracts.
Pressure to open the platform widely vs. protecting SAP's core application ecosystem advantage.

assumption_rationale: High structural conflict from internal SAP units protective of their data domains and revenue streams.
Significant drag from reallocating engineering and sales resources from legacy BDC to the new integrated vision.
Moderate cultural clash between SAP's process-centric culture and Reltio's agile, cloud-native DNA.

modeling_notes: This scenario bets on SAP's ability to execute a bold, market-making move.
Success hinges on overcoming internal inertia and changing the customer conversation from migration risk to AI opportunity.
The primary rival is market inertia and customer skepticism, not a specific competitor.

scenarios.title: Defensive Survival: De-risking BDC Adoption and Migration
scenarios.goal: Ensure BDC's survival and compliance with hard market constraints by systematically eliminating adoption barriers.
scenarios.objective: Use the Reltio acquisition primarily to address the critical 'pay-during-migration' constraint and rebuild credibility, prioritizing safe adoption over market leadership.

variables.name: Migration risk reduction score
variables.description: Measured reduction in perceived and actual financial risk for customers during BDC migration.

variables.name: Customer familiarity with BDC
variables.description: Percentage of DSAG members reporting moderate or high familiarity post-Reltio integration messaging.

variables.name: Integration completeness for core SAP apps
variables.description: Depth and reliability of Reltio-powered data harmonization for SAP's own major applications.

constraints: Must address customer concerns about paying for unusable systems during migration
Must overcome low customer familiarity with BDC (83% of DSAG members)
Must integrate data from outside SAP's broad application portfolio
tradeoff_pressure: Pressure to invest in perfecting migration safety vs. allocating resources to offensive AI feature development.
Pressure to simplify messaging to rebuild trust vs. accurately communicating the complex technical advantages of AI-native MDM.
Pressure to meet immediate customer demands for safety vs. building the long-term, open data platform vision.

assumption_rationale: Lower structural conflict as defensive goal aligns with sales and customer support units feeling pain from BDC failures.
Very high resource drag from creating new migration tools, guarantees, and support processes.
High veto node intensity from legal and finance teams wary of offering success-based fees and guarantees.

modeling_notes: This is a compliance and survival scenario. Market leadership is a secondary concern.
The primary constraint network is the set of customer objections and market sentiments documented in the signals.
Success is measured by stopping the bleeding and stabilizing BDC's reputation.

scenarios.title: Direct Confrontation: Winning the Enterprise Data Hub War
scenarios.goal: Compete directly with other data platform vendors (Databricks, Snowflake, Informatica) for the role of central enterprise data hub.
scenarios.target: Market share in the enterprise data integration and MDM segment.
scenarios.objective: Leverage the combined SAP-Reltio stack to win head-to-head deals against rival platforms, under established rules of feature competition and total cost of ownership.

variables.name: Competitive win rate
variables.description: Percentage of competitive deals against named rivals where SAP-Reltio wins.

variables.name: Feature parity/advantage index
variables.description: Comparison of key capabilities (entity resolution, connectivity, AI readiness) against primary competitors.

variables.name: Total cost of ownership (TCO) advantage
variables.description: Quantified TCO benefit presented to prospects versus competing platforms.

constraints: Must integrate data from outside SAP's broad application portfolio
Must maintain Reltio's cloud-native approach to data integration and MDM
Must leverage Reltio's AI-based entity resolution system for golden records
tradeoff_pressure: Pressure to match competitor feature velocity vs. maintaining deep integration with the SAP application suite.
Pressure to compete on price/TCO vs. protecting the premium value of SAP's enterprise ecosystem.
Pressure to sell the combined platform as a standalone product vs. using it primarily as an AI enabler for SAP apps.

assumption_rationale: High structural conflict with SAP's traditional sales motion focused on its own applications.
Moderate drag from building a dedicated competitive sales and marketing team for the data platform war.
Moderate cultural misalignment with Reltio's team now being asked to compete in SAP's large, complex deal cycles.

modeling_notes: This scenario treats the market as a rule-bounded, resource-competitive arena with clear rivals.
The rival network includes other MDM vendors, data lakehouses, and integration platform as a service (iPaaS) players.
Success is defined by taking measurable share from specific competitors.
